gap_summaries.title: sandbox_env extras can override CHILD_FORCED_VARS and re-introduce CHILD_STRIPPED_VARS
gap_summaries.description: sandbox_env applied extras AFTER stripping security vars and forcing the child flag, so extras={"STORY_AUTOMATOR_CHILD": "false"} could disable the trust boundary, and extras={"BMAD_AUDIT_KEY": "x"} could re-expose the audit signing key to the child. Reordered to apply extras before strip+force so security invariants always win.

production_readiness.security: pass — no secrets in code/logs, file paths sanitized, trust boundary guards on all write ops
production_readiness.error_resilience: pass — all subprocess/file I/O calls handle errors, best-effort cleanup on failure paths
production_readiness.testing: pass — 1831 tests green, no test-level sleeps, no network calls, strong assertions throughout
production_readiness.code_quality: pass — no TODO/FIXME/HACK, follows CLAUDE.md conventions, no circular imports
production_readiness.package_hygiene: pass — no new dependencies, all modules importable, __all__ exports defined

verification.npm_verify: test:python, pack:dry-run, test:cli, test:smoke — all passed
